name: asparagus mint cream pasta
instructions:
Boil pasta and asparagus together in the same pot of salted water. Reserve a generous amount of the cooking water before draining — it's used in the sauce.

Meanwhile, sauté yellow onion and garlic in olive oil over medium heat until softened.

Build the sauce by adding heavy whipping cream and a generous splash of the reserved pasta water to the onion/garlic. Simmer briefly to combine. The pasta water + cream is the body of the sauce.

For the mint: rather than chopping raw leaves into the sauce, infuse mint into the reserved pasta water before adding it to the cream. Drop the mint leaves into the hot pasta water for a few minutes, strain out, then use that infused water in the sauce. Alternatively, use chopped mint leaves very sparingly in the finished sauce — raw chopped mint can read bitter at volume.

Toss the drained pasta and asparagus into the sauce. Finish with grated parmesan, black pepper, and salt to taste.
notes:
From Samin Nosrat (Salt Fat Acid Heat), with one modification.

MINT — INFUSION OVER CHOPPED: Samin's original calls for freshly chopped mint leaves in the sauce. I find chopped mint reads bitter at the quantity the dish wants. Infusing mint into the hot pasta water (steeping it briefly, then straining) gives you the mint flavor without the bitterness. If you do go with chopped raw mint, use it very sparingly.

SLICE THE ASPARAGUS THINNER THAN YOU EXPECT: cut on the long diagonal or into thin coins. Thick asparagus pieces don't twirl with the pasta and end up as separate components on the plate. The asparagus should integrate with the pasta strand-by-strand, which means smaller/thinner than instinct suggests.

PASTA WATER + CREAM: this is the sauce backbone. The starchy pasta water carries body without needing additional thickener; the cream adds richness. Don't skip the pasta water — pure cream alone reads too heavy.

ALLIUMS: the yellow onion + garlic combination is the soffrito base. No tricks — soften them in olive oil before the cream goes in.
